summary: Terminal attach commands, shell sessions, mail alerts in Terminal, and more reliable clipboard hotkey.

introduced: Terminal workspace lists each active tmux session with a copy-paste attach command that unsets TMUX to avoid nested-session warnings
New Session can start a normal shell or a new tmux session
changed: Incoming mail alerts appear as the same modal overlay while you stay in Terminal instead of switching back to Dashboard
Terminal workspace no longer auto-returns to Dashboard after idle time
fixed: Global Shift-Command-V clipboard picker registers a Carbon hotkey so it works from Cursor and other apps
Menu bar Paste from Clipboard History context menu shortcut uses the correct lowercase key equivalent
